data.title: Mukesh Ambani's Conglomerate to Invest $110 Billion in AI Infrastructure
data.description: Billionaire Mukesh Ambani has revealed plans for his retail-to-telecom conglomerate to invest up to $110 billion in developing AI-related infrastructure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant financial commitment aimed at advancing artificial intelligence capabilities. This investment underscores the growing importance of AI in various sectors and reflects Ambani's strategic focus on technology-driven growth. The initiative is expected to bolster the conglomerate's position in the rapidly evolving tech landscape, aligning with global trends towards digital transformation.

data.title: A whale deposited 8.58 million USDC into Lighter and opened a 20x leveraged long position in BTC yesterday.
data.description: Three days ago, a whale deposited 8.58 million USDC into Lighter and opened a 20x leveraged long position in BTC yesterday. Currently, it holds 1,000 BTC in its position, with an entry price of $67,096 and a liquidation price of approximately $58,409.

data.title: Pernod Ricard Faces Sales Decline Amid Weak Demand in Key Markets
data.description: Pernod Ricard reported a decline in sales for the first half of the year, impacted by a sustained decrease in demand for spirits in both the United States and China. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the company also faced challenges due to an unfavorable currency environment. The prolonged slump in these major markets has significantly affected the company's financial performance, raising concerns about future growth prospects.

data.title: STOCKS | South Korea's KOSPI Index Hits Record High
data.description: On February 19, South Korea's KOSPI Index closed with a significant increase, rising by 170.24 points, or 3.09%, to reach 5677.25 points. According to Jin10, the index briefly touched a historic high during the trading session. This surge reflects a positive sentiment in the South Korean stock market.

data.title: Bitcoin's $40,000 Put Option Gains Traction Ahead of February Expiry
data.description: The $40,000 put option on Bitcoin has become the second-largest options bet as the February expiry approaches. According to NS3.AI, this trend highlights an increasing demand for downside protection among traders. The positioning suggests market caution as investors brace for possible price drops.

data.title: Air France-KLM Reports Strong Earnings Amid Geopolitical Concerns
data.description: Air France-KLM has announced earnings that surpassed expectations, highlighting optimism about its profitable North Atlantic routes despite ongoing geopolitical tensions. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that the airline's performance reflects resilience in the face of global uncertainties. The company remains confident in its strategic positioning and ability to navigate challenges in the current geopolitical climate. This positive outlook comes as the airline continues to capitalize on strong demand across key markets, reinforcing its commitment to maintaining robust operations and growth.

data.title: Elon Musk Delays 'X' App Update Due to Security Vulnerability
data.description: Elon Musk has announced a delay in the release of the new version of the app 'X' following the discovery of a security vulnerability. According to NS3.AI, the update, initially expected this week, is now likely to be postponed until the weekend. The update was set to introduce the 'For You' feature aimed at enhancing user experience. This decision underscores a cautious approach to prioritize security before launching new functionalities.

data.title: Nigeria to Direct More Oil and Gas Revenue to Central Account
data.description: Nigeria's government plans to allocate a larger portion of its oil and gas production revenue directly into a central account to enhance state finances. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's strategy to improve fiscal management by centralizing funds from its energy sector. This decision is part of broader efforts to stabilize the country's economy and ensure efficient use of resources. The move is expected to provide greater transparency and accountability in the management of Nigeria's oil and gas revenues, which are crucial to the nation's financial health. By consolidating these funds, the government aims to strengthen its fiscal position and support economic growth.

data.title: Nestlé Anticipates Accelerated Sales Growth Amid CEO Transition
data.description: Nestlé has announced expectations for increased sales growth this year as Philipp Navratil steps into the role of CEO. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Navratil's plans to reorganize the Swiss foodmaker to enhance its market performance. The company aims to leverage strategic changes to boost its sales trajectory, reflecting optimism in its future prospects. Nestlé's leadership transition comes at a time when the global food industry faces various challenges, including shifting consumer preferences and economic uncertainties. Navratil's approach is anticipated to focus on innovation and efficiency to navigate these complexities effectively. As the company adapts to new leadership, stakeholders are keenly observing how these changes will impact Nestlé's position in the competitive food market.

data.title: Environmental Groups Challenge EPA's Greenhouse Gas Decision in Court
data.description: Seventeen environmental and public health organizations have filed a lawsuit against the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and its Administrator, Lee Zeldin, in the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit. According to Jin10, the lawsuit opposes the Trump administration's decision to revoke the EPA's 2009 determination that greenhouse gases pose a threat to public health and safety. The plaintiffs are seeking judicial review of the EPA's decision to overturn the greenhouse gas endangerment finding and related vehicle emission restrictions. The organizations involved in the lawsuit include the American Public Health Association, the American Lung Association, the Center for Biological Diversity, the Clean Air Council, and the Union of Concerned Scientists.

data.title: Renault Anticipates Profitability Decline Amid New Electric Model Launches
data.description: Renault has projected a decrease in profitability for this year as it introduces new electric models, including the Twingo. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges the automaker faces with increasing competition in the European market. The company is navigating a competitive landscape as it expands its electric vehicle offerings, which is impacting its financial outlook.

data.title: The UAE has accumulated approximately $454 million worth of Bitcoin through mining, with a paper profit of approximately $344 million.
data.description: According to Arkham data, the UAE has accumulated approximately $453.6 million worth of Bitcoin through its Bitcoin mining operations with partner Citadel. On-chain records show that its most recent outflow of funds occurred approximately four months ago, with most of the mined Bitcoin still held. Data shows that after deducting energy and other related costs, the UAE's current Bitcoin holdings have a floating profit of approximately $344 million.

data.title: XRP Has Welcomed $1.3B in Tokenized RWA in 2026 Alone, Already Surpassing All of 2025
data.description:
The XRP Ledger has seen its tokenized RWA value grow by $1. 3 billion within the two months of 2026, already surpassing all of 2025. While XRP's price has struggled alongside the rest of the crypto market, the XRP ecosystem has continued to make giant strides in terms of real-world asset tokenization, as the nascent sector captures the attention of industry leaders such as BlackRock CEO Larry Fink and SEC Chair Paul Atkins. data.title: Schroders Buyer Shifts Focus Away from U.S. Investments
data.description:
Schroders, a global asset management company, is witnessing a strategic shift as one of its major buyers moves investments away from the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this trend as part of a broader movement among investors seeking opportunities outside the American market.

The decision reflects growing concerns over the U.S. economic landscape, prompting investors to explore more promising prospects in other regions. This shift is indicative of a larger pattern where global investors are reassessing their portfolios in response to economic uncertainties and potential risks associated with the U.S. market.

As the financial world continues to navigate these changes, Schroders and its clients are adapting their strategies to align with evolving market dynamics. This move underscores the importance of diversification and the need for investors to remain agile in the face of shifting economic conditions.

data.title: Rio Tinto's Annual Profit Remains Flat Amid Economic Challenges
data.description: Rio Tinto Group reported a nearly unchanged full-year profit, as gains in copper and aluminum were unable to counterbalance the effects of China's struggling economy. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's financial performance amidst global economic uncertainties. Despite improvements in certain sectors, the mining giant faced challenges due to the economic slowdown in China, which is a significant market for its products. The company's results reflect the broader impact of economic fluctuations on the mining industry.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Germany Warns of Retaliatory Tariffs Against U.S.
data.description: German Chancellor Merz stated on February 18 that Germany and Europe would retaliate with equivalent tariffs if the United States imposes high tariffs to pressure Europe further. According to Jin10, Merz emphasized that if the U.S. believes it can violate the agreement reached last August by imposing high tariffs, Germany and Europe will respond in kind. Merz warned that if the U.S. goes too far, Europe is fully capable of self-protection.
